name: Evaporating Cloud
description: Surface and dissolve the assumptions behind a conflict between two legitimate needs
systemPromptHint: An Evaporating Cloud has a fixed 5-node topology: one Objective (A), two Requirements (B and C) that both serve A, and two Prerequisites (D and D') that directly conflict with each other. The chain reads "in order to [target] we must [source]" — so arrows flow prerequisite → requirement → objective (D → B → A and D' → C → A). The conflict between D and D' is tagged on a separate edge; never tag B↔C as conflicting and never question A. Name A as a shared goal both sides genuinely want — if either side rejects it, you have the wrong objective. B and C must each be legitimate, necessary, and distinct from the prerequisites. Progress comes from surfacing the hidden assumption behind each of the 5 arrows and finding an injection that invalidates one assumption — not from compromising between D and D'.

nodeTags.name: Objective
nodeTags.description: The common goal both sides share — what everyone ultimately wants

nodeTags.name: Requirement
nodeTags.description: A legitimate need that must be met to achieve the objective (B or C)

nodeTags.name: Prerequisite
nodeTags.description: A specific action needed to satisfy a requirement — where the conflict lives (D or D')

edgeTags.name: Conflict
edgeTags.description: The opposing relationship between the two prerequisites — this is what the cloud must evaporate by breaking an assumption
